author: redoctober
text: Liverpool 2026/27 Season - Iraola Officially Confirmed21 minutes agoReplyQuoteSelect PostDeselect PostLink to PostMemberGive GiftBack to TopPost by redoctober on21 minutes ago14 hours agoMolbyJockeyGerardsaid:Realistically the outlook under Iraola, would be;Bed down an identity, system that makes Liverpool FC competitive (offensively and defensively). First and foremost. That could take the better part of 6 months, could be sooner.A top 4 finish in the EPL, and as far as the progress of the squad allows in the CL. But this is based purely on the progress in the latter half of next season. (ensuring a unified, cohesive base to springboard from).That said, we make Anfield roar again, a fortress of note, become engage in battle ...Agreed.  Expectations should be tempered.  It'll take time for him to get a new approach embedded.  We also need to sign a lot of players to replenish the squad and fill holes in the first team.  Will take time for those players to bed in too.  You would hope that players like Wirtz, Isak, Kerkez and Frimpong will be a bit better this coming season as they've had time to adjust to the Club/League etc.  Top 4 would be decent but optimistically we might do better and maybe a run in the CL depending on how our draw goes.  If we got a draw like Arsenal we could win it!I think we should be optimistic.  We've got rid of someone who seemed quite negative, glass-half-empty and didn't seem to connect with the players or fans.  We've now got someone who's young and ambitious, has a passion for what he's doing and seems to realise how big Liverpool is and what's expected.  He's coming off the back of doing some good things with a smaller club and implementing some exciting attacking football despite losing top players and having limited budget.Looking back to the time Rafa came in, there are some similarities.  We had a team that finished top 4 but needed upgrading despite signing some big players not long before.  Rafa brought in a couple of gems in Alonso and Garcia.  We were poor enough in the league of course.  It'll be interesting to see who we bring in this summer.  Everything really hinges on that I think.  While we have some quality players right now and I'm sure Iraola will get more from them, we've got holes to fill such as RW, DM and RB at the least.  If we can get some decent signings for those positions I'd fancy us to have an alright season.
